codexCorrections.reason: KnowledgeMappingV4 explicitly permits the narrower relation; each point has a page reference, a canonical parent concept and a granularity note.

issues: Function-notation node captures f(x) but the broader algebraic vocabulary (constant, coefficient, identity, index, etc.) is only loosely covered by the use-of-symbols node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Retain mapping; consider adding a broader algebraic-notation parent if granularity allows.
No change required.

issues: The source requires addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of polynomials, but the mapped nodes only cover division by linear/quadratic expressions and cubic/quartic polynomials.
No node is mapped for general polynomial addition, subtraction or multiplication.
pointReviews.recommendation: Add the ALGF-POLY-POLY parent node or equivalent broader polynomial-operations node, and retain division nodes.
No change required.

Keep. PT6 maps acceptably to trigonometric modelling and Pythagoras nodes; the modelling node is a parent but appropriate for applied 2D/3D problems.
Keep. Differentiating kx^n and sums is well covered by the power rule and constant-multiple/sum/difference nodes.
Keep. Direct match to derivative-as-gradient and derivative-as-rate-of-change concept nodes.
Keep. Gradient at a point links naturally to the derivative-as-gradient concept and the tangents application node.

issues: Inclusion of the parent CALC-INTE-RULE node is redundant alongside the specific CALC-INTE-RULE-POWR node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep, but consider removing the redundant parent node CALC-INTE-RULE.
Keep. Exact match to integration as reverse differentiation.
Keep. Exact matches to indefinite and definite integral concept nodes.
Keep. Evaluating definite integrals is directly covered by the definite integrals node.
Keep. Exact match to area under a curve.
Keep. Exact match to area between curves.
Keep. Variable-acceleration kinematics is comprehensively mapped to the calculus-in-kinematics displacement/velocity/acceleration nodes.

issues: Inclusion of the parent CALC-KIN-SVAT node is redundant alongside the specific MECH-KIN-LINE-CONST node.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep, but consider removing the redundant parent node CALC-KIN-SVAT.
Keep. Exact match to locating roots by sign change.
Keep. Simple iterative methods are adequately represented by the iteration formulae node.
Keep. Failure of sign-change methods and iteration convergence together cover recognition of method failure.

issues: The specification requires using a chord to estimate the gradient of a tangent, a numerical technique. The canonical node CALC-DIFF-CONC-LIMT ('gradient as a limit') captures the underlying concept but not the chord-based estimation procedure itself.
pointReviews.recommendation: Review. The current fit is acceptable only because no dedicated numerical-gradient-estimation node exists, but the mapping is weak.

issues: The specification requires improving an estimate for the gradient of a curve at a point. The canonical node CALC-DIFF-CONC-LIMT relates to refining estimates through limits conceptually, but does not specifically represent improving a chord-based numerical estimate.
pointReviews.recommendation: Review. The current fit is acceptable only because no dedicated numerical-gradient-estimation node exists, but the mapping is weak.
Keep. Rectangular-strip estimation is appropriately mapped to the numerical integration parent node in the absence of a dedicated rectangular-strip node.
Keep. Direct match to trapezium rule estimate and approximate area under a curve nodes.
Keep. Over/under-estimate for the trapezium rule directly matches the corresponding node.

issues: Mapping uses broad parent nodes (NUMM-ROOT, NUMM-NINT, REAS-MODL-MODL) rather than specific technique nodes.
pointReviews.recommendation: Keep, but prefer more specific child nodes where the applied context is known.
Keep. Exact match to the graph and properties of a to the x node.
Keep. Exact match to logarithms as inverse functions.
Keep. Exact match to laws of logarithms.
Keep. Reduction to linear form is adequately represented by the combination of logarithm laws and exponential models nodes.
Keep. Estimating parameters from log-linear graphs is reasonably mapped to exponential models and line-of-best-fit nodes.

issues: Source reference Page 18, EL6 is supported, but the official specification rendering for EL6 is garbled/broken (the equation form and condition are not cleanly legible in the supplied text), so the exact wording of the point cannot be independently verified.

The mapping to ALGF-LOG-LOGR-EQNS (Exponential and logarithmic equations) is semantically appropriate for an EL6 item about solving exponential equations, but the claimed 'exact' relation rests on an unclear source line.
pointReviews.recommendation: Verify the exact official wording of EL6 from a clean source; retain the node mapping if the point concerns solving exponential/logarithmic equations.
No action required.

overallIssues.issue: AL3 is under-mapped: the source requires all polynomial operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication and division), but only division and degree-specific nodes are mapped.
CG4 uses parent graph nodes; more specific child nodes for linear, polynomial and trigonometric graphs are available and would improve granularity.

NM4 and NM5 (chord-based gradient estimation and improving estimates) have weak canonical node fits because the catalog lacks dedicated numerical-gradient-estimation nodes. They are currently mapped to the limit-concept node CALC-DIFF-CONC-LIMT, which captures the underlying idea but not the specified numerical procedure.

Several points include redundant parent nodes alongside specific child nodes (e.g., CA8 includes CALC-INTE-RULE with CALC-INTE-RULE-POWR; CA15 includes CALC-KIN-SVAT with MECH-KIN-LINE-CONST). These are not incorrect but reduce precision.

NM9 is mapped to broad parent nodes. This is acceptable for a generic 'apply numerical methods in context' statement, but more specific child nodes should be used when the contextual technique is identifiable.
The supplied official specification text renders Page 18, EL6 with broken equation formatting, creating uncertainty about the precise point wording.

Three Numerical Methods syllabus points rely solely on a 'narrower' canonical mapping, leaving their full official scope unrepresented and creating a coverage gap in numerical differentiation and numerical integration.
